methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $1.8B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Watershed
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Panthalassa is a Series A climate and energy technology company. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of four same-sector, same-stage private companies: Watershed ($1.8B), Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M). This sample represents a portion of a broader comparable universe that includes eight private frontier-tech peers and one public company (ticker: LNZA).

The peer-median method is applicable and yields a median valuation of $175M across the four identified comparables. The peer set exhibits substantial dispersion, with valuations ranging from $110M at the low end to $1.8B at the high end, reflecting heterogeneity in business model, deployment stage, and investor appetite within the climate and energy sector at Series A. This wide range ($110M–$1.8B) signals material uncertainty in fair value estimation for companies at this stage.

The triangulated valuation range is $149M to $201M, centered at $175M. This reflects application of a single method (peer-median), which limits the robustness of the estimate. Key uncertainties include Panthalassa's relative technology maturity, capital efficiency, market addressability, and commercial traction relative to peers. The large gap between the minimum and maximum peer valuations underscores the sensitivity of this estimate to peer selection and suggests that transaction-specific factors will likely dominate final pricing.
